Humane plans to dissolve its customer support team for the AI Pin on February 28.
The company says customers who bought an AI pin in the last 90 days are eligible for a refund, according to an FAQ, but anyone who bought a device before then is not.
The Bay Area startup, founded by ex-Apple employees Bethany Bongiorno and Imran Chaudhri, raised more than $230 million to create the device.
At one point last summer, Humane’s returns for the AI Pin started outpacing its sales, according to reporting from The Verge.
This technology could be used to integrate AI into HP’s personal computers and printers."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/humane-ai-pin-discontinued-killed","title":"Humane has killed its Ai Pin less than a year after its release | Mashable","content":"In a post to Humane's support website, the company announced that all of its Ai Pins will stop working in just 10 days.
15 are eligible for a refund, as they'll still be within the 90-day return window.
"Humane’s AI platform Cosmos, backed by an incredible group of engineers, will help us create an intelligent ecosystem across all HP devices from AI PCs to smart printers and connected conference rooms," HP's President of Technology and Innovation Tuan Tran said in a statement.
Though Humane quickly updated the clip, numerous other problems with the Ai Pin soon became apparent once users started getting their hands on the device in April last year.

All refunds must be submitted by February 27th, 2025.”","score":0.8051581,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reddit.com/r/humane/comments/1itkjxi/humanes_ai_pin_shutdown_unfair_to_customers","title":"Humane's Ai Pin Shutdown – Unfair to Customers!","content":"Humane’s Ai Pin Shutdown – Unfair to Customers!\nr/humane - 47 upvotes, 40 comments\nI purchased the Humane Ai Pin around $1,000 on 9/20/24, expecting an innovative, long-term device. Five month later, Humane is shutting down all services, making the device completely useless by February 28, 2025. What’s worse? They refuse to issue refunds, even though: - My original order was never fully fulfilled. The charger case was missing due to safety issues, and I was forced to wait 3-6 months for it. - When they finally offered a refund or a 3-6-month wait , I chose to wait—but now, with Ai Pin being discontinued, I’m stuck with nothing. - Despite my order not being complete, they are using a 90-day policy against me! No Refunds for a Dead Device? Humane refuses to take responsibility, hiding behind a strict 90-day return policy, even though the device is being bricked far before its expected lifespan. This isn’t a customer’s fault—it’s a failure of Humane’s business model, and paying customers are being left with expensive e-waste. At noon Pacific time on Feb. 28, all Humane AI Pins sold will stop connecting to servers and cease to work. HP has agreed to take over the company's assets in a deal valued at roughly $116 million, while the AI Pin dreams will be abandoned. There is no word why the product has been canceled, and what exactly HP intends to do with the acquisition. Humane has put a refund policy in place for the AI Pin as well as the charging case, but the terms will leave early adopters fuming.
